summary.brief: Potter v. South. Medical malpractice case involving psychiatry.

evidence_index.text: Kevin Potter suffered injuries related to a medical procedure—a celiac plexus block—performed by Dr. South on January 9, 2019, at Mercy Health.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The Butler County Court of Common Pleas granted summary judgment to the Defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court did not err by granting summary judgment to the Defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Potters attached to their complaint an 'Affidavit of Merit' signed by Dr. Vladimir Kushnir, dated June 2, 2020.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: We overrule the Potters' sole assignment of error.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Potters failed to disclose any expert witnesses by the court's deadline.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Summary judgment is a procedural device used to terminate litigation when there is no genuine issue of any material fact.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's analysis

evidence_index.text: The Potters' new trial counsel did not appear for the hearing but arranged for substitute counsel to appear in his place.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The trial court correctly granted summary judgment to the Defendants.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: The Potters again attached Dr. Kushnir's June 2, 2020 Affidavit of Merit to the new complaint.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding
